steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the overall structure of the two-sentence text. The first sentence makes a broad claim about high-speed rail expanding in many countries. The second sentence, signaled by “for instance,” gives specific figures for Morocco. The key task is naming the organizational pattern those two parts form.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The text notes a general trend and then cites an example of that trend.: Sentence 1 states the general trend (high-speed rail expanding globally); sentence 2 cites Morocco as a specific example with concrete numbers. The phrase “for instance” explicitly marks this relationship. ✓
  • Choice B — The text summarizes an argument and then offers a challenge to that argument.: No challenge or counterargument appears; Morocco’s data supports, not undermines, the opening claim. ✗
  • Choice C — The text questions a course of action and then suggests alternatives to that action.: The text does not question high-speed rail or propose any alternative course of action. ✗
  • Choice D — The text rejects a long-held belief and then proposes a different interpretation.: No belief is rejected, and no reinterpretation is offered. The text presents high-speed rail expansion as a positive trend, not as a correction of a prior view. ✗
A is correct.
